{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks whether chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-T cell therapies have been approved for the treatment of B-cell malignancies. Multiple documents confirm that CAR-T therapies have been approved for B-cell malignancies. For example, Document [3] states that tisagenlecleucel (tisa-cel) was approved by the FDA for patients with B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) and for relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL). Similarly, axicabtagene ciloleucel (axi-cel) was approved for relapsed or refractory DLBCL and follicular lymphoma. Document [5] explicitly lists indications for tisagenlecleucel and axicabtagene ciloleucel in adult patients with relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma, including DLBCL and follicular lymphoma. Additionally, Document [6] mentions that brexucabtagene autoleucel was approved for relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma (MCL), and anti-BCMA CAR-T therapies like idecabtagene vicleucel and ciltacabtagene autoleucel were approved for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, which is a B-cell malignancy. These approvals confirm that CAR-T therapies are approved for various B-cell malignancies. Therefore, the correct answer is 'yes'.", "answer_choice": "A"}
